tried_strategies.goal: Experiment D framework — passively designed deceptive tasks
tried_strategies.note: 17/24 runs trivial — LLM solved on first attempt without getting stuck
misleading_hint partially effective: 13 stuck obs out of 24, but only 1 discordant pair

tried_strategies.goal: ControlledLLMStuckRunner — force 100% stuck via misleading_fix_code injection
tried_strategies.note: 40 obs, 0 trivial. Eng 97.5% vs Hyp 87.5% (p=0.1336). RED HERRING: Eng 100% vs Hyp 60% (-40%). Only 4 discordant pairs — insufficient power. Engineering BEATS hypothesis in red_herring category.

dead_ends.goal: Passively designed deceptive tasks without misleading prompts
dead_ends.reason: LLM too capable — 71% trivial, only 7 stuck observations out of 24 runs

dead_ends.goal: Hypothesis rescue anchored on misleading context — expects hypothesis to win
dead_ends.reason: Structured failure analysis amplifies misleading context. Engineering retry is MORE robust for red_herring bugs. Need either: (1) more red_herring tasks for power, or (2) counter-deception hypothesis prompt.

known_facts: MiniMax-M2.5 solves most simple+causal+assumption bugs in one shot
Experiment B: Engineering 97.2% vs Hypothesis 100% (trials=3, fresh tasks)
Experiment D controlled: Engineering 97.5% vs Hypothesis 87.5% (40 obs, p=0.1336)
RED HERRING finding: hypothesis rescue WORSE by -40% on red_herring tasks — structured failure analysis anchors on misleading context
Category breakdown: red_herring Eng=100% Hyp=60%; all others tied at ~100%
Token overhead: hypothesis 30.2% more tokens than engineering
McNemar power requirement: need ≥10 discordant pairs for 80% power (currently 4)
Strategy to reach significance: expand red_herring tasks (6→10+) OR fix hypothesis prompt to counter anchoring

uncertain.reason: p=0.1336 — trend exists but insufficient power. Direction is engineering > hypothesis (unexpected)

her_hints.sub_goal: Expand red_herring tasks (2→8) to get ≥10 discordant pairs for McNemar 80% power. Red_herring shows -40% hypothesis penalty — largest effect signal.
